text:
Can we stop glorifying being unhealthy in Corporate?

Late night != Hustling, Hardworking
Late night = Messed up Sleeping Schedule

Skipping meals != Dedication
Skipping Meals = Bad Health

No time for gym/health != Busy, Productivity
No time for gym/health = Health Issues

Always being available != Reliable
Always being available = No personal life

Constant multitasking != Smart working
Constant multitasking = Scattered focus

Canceling plans with family/friends != Career focus
Canceling plans = Losing the life you’re working for

Because one day, you’ll realize you built a career… but forgot to build a life around it.

LinkedIn has become that one overachieving relative at every family function.

'Sharma ji ka beta got a job of 19084692369 INR what are YOU doing?'

Every post is either:

'My 48 start-ups failed but I never gave up'

or 

'I made 3 crores at 21, here's how can you too'

No one posts:

'I am confused'
'I don't know what I am doing'
'I cried yesterday for no reason'
'I applied to 180 jobs and got ghosted by all'

Because that thing - won't get you likes, won't get you comments.

So, we pretend, we all become - thought leaders, humbled learners, hustlers, philosphers..

LinkedIn is the only place where you are either building a start-up or building a personal brand or building anxiety. There is no 4th option.

Here is what i want you to know -

Most people are still figuring it out.
Most people are lost.
Most people are just trying to get through the day without feeling like a failure.

And that is raw, that is okay.

Till then, please read how waking up at 5 am changed my life stories on LinkedIn, it is okay if you are going to read it at 2 am, questioning your existence - because that my friend is also a part of life

Next time when I'll be reading some LinkedIn Influencer's story, I am starting from the last line.
If there's a link attached to it, it's most probably a fake one.
Saves me time!

Your LinkedIn feed is lying to you.
Not because people are fake.

But because nobody posts the middle.

You’ll see:

Offer letter
Promotion
New role

But you’ll never see:

The 47 rejections
The silent breakdowns
The “am I even good enough?” phase

Success looks loud online.
But in reality,
it’s built in very quiet, very uncomfortable phases
no one claps for.

If you’re in that phase right now,
you’re not behind.
You’re just not posting it.

We spend half our lives thinking -
’Once I get there, I’ll be happy.’

But there is no ‘there.’
Every goal you hit shifts the finish line.
Maybe the real success is not in arrival,
but in learning to love the walk - even when it’s uphill.

Coding is dead.

Google search is dead.

Data analysis is dead.

Indian IT industry is dead.

SaaS is dead.

RAG is dead.

...

Influencers have been killing things non-stop since ChatGPT launched.

‘Mujhe bahut paisa kamaane hai’ - but do we stop and think what it might cost us?

It costs your mental peace
It costs your sleep
It costs your relationships
It costs your weekends
It costs the version of you that used to feel excited about life

And the funny part?
No one tells you this before you start chasing it.

Jabb aap zindagi ki race mein bahut tez bhaagne lagg jaate ho aur bahut dur aa jate ho, tabb peeche ka sabb dhundhla lagne lagta hai.

On LinkedIn, success is aesthetic.

People post offer letters
Not anxiety attacks

People post promotions
Not panic at 2 AM

People post “Grateful & Humbled”
Not “Exhausted & Numb”

And slowly, without realizing…
You start believing that something is wrong with you.
“Why am I not happy even after getting what I wanted?”

The truth is - You never wanted that high paying job, yuou wanted the feeling you thought it would give you.

Security
Validation
Respect
Peace

And when the job doesn’t give you that…
You feel cheated.

So you chase the next thing.
Better salary
Better company
Better title
Hoping this time it will fix everything.

But it doesn’t.
Because the problem was never outside.

It’s the way we’ve been taught to define success.
We were never taught how to be okay with ourselves
Without achievements
Without validation
Without constantly proving something

So now we’re stuck in this loop:
Achieve → Feel empty → Chase more → Repeat
And the worst part?
From the outside, it looks like you’re “winning”

If you’re feeling this right now…
You’re not ungrateful
You’re not lazy
You’re not broken
You’re just tired of chasing a life that was never designed for your happiness.

Maybe it’s time we stop asking:
’What’s the next milestone?’

And start asking:
’Do I even like the life I’m building?’

Because success that costs your peace…
Is just a well-packaged burnout.'

Every time I poured my heart into 5-6 rounds of interviews and faced rejection, it felt like a punch in the gut. The sleepless nights, the endless preparation, all for nothing.

But looking back, I realize it wasn’t nothing. It was the Universe’s way of saying, “Not this one, something better is on the way.”

Every single time, I’ve been shown why that rejection happened.

Doors I thought I wanted to walk through were shut, only to have the right ones swing open.

The kind that aligned with my growth, my values, and my happiness.

At first, it stung. It hurt deeply. But now, when things don’t go as planned, I don’t panic.

I don’t question my worth. I sit back, breathe, and trust. The Universe knows.

I know there's another plan waiting. Something bigger, better, and just for me.

To anyone feeling the weight of rejection: trust that the closed doors are protecting you from something you can’t see right now.

Your path is being cleared for something even more beautiful.

To everyone who's still looking for a job...

I see you. I feel you. 

Every rejection email feels like a punch in the gut, and every 'We'll get back to you' sounds more like 'You'll never hear from us.'

But I want you to know, you're not alone in this. 

According to a study, 80% of jobseekers struggle with anxiety and self-doubt during their search. It's normal to feel lost, but it's not the end.

Take breaks, breathe, and remember, this doesn't define you. Your worth is not tied to an offer letter. 

Your mental health matters more than any job.

Sometimes, we forget that a company’s brand name doesn’t define someone’s talent. It’s easy to get caught up in the 'big company = big talent' mindset, but that's not always the case.

I’ve had the privilege of working with people from smaller companies (lesser known) who blow me away with their skills and dedication. They don’t need a fancy title or a famous brand behind them to prove their worth.

I've seen the other side too—people in top-tier companies feeling lost, overwhelmed, or stuck, even though the world sees them as 'successful'.

Let’s stop attaching someone’s value to the company they work for. Freshers especially need to hear this—skills are what matter, not the size of the company behind them.

At the end of the day, happiness and growth don’t come from a brand name, they come from doing what you love and constantly improving your craft.

So when I left a toxic work environment, I told my manager a simple thing and felt so good 

I just said-

'Hope your son gets a manager like you.
I hope that the manager behaves the same way as you did with me.
Thank you.'

Now tell me 1 thing-

She always said that she was a great manager.
Why will she get offended?

I just told her that I wish her son would get a manager like she was.

If you felt bad, then that means you were a bad manager and now you know it. 

If you feel good, then take it as a blessing for your son and you'll really want someone to treat your son/daughter in the same way.

She cannot be even angry with me else it'll prove that she was not a 'great' manager.

Muskan - 1
Manager - 0

Muskan -> Aura +100000000

(Fictional message unfortunately :(
)

Hope you all become the people that your sons/daughters will like to work under 

There are a lot of bad people/things, bring a small change and break the chain :)

LinkedIn scams be like: 'Congratulations, you've been selected for a role you didn’t even apply for!' 
 The catch? Pay Rs. 50,000 for the honor.

sapne dekhna achi baat hai,
lekin job ka sapna dekh ke 'interested' likhna,
yeh toh achi baat nahi hai na?
